topic: My security camera records 47 seconds of footage I never saw happen every night at 2:13 AM
title: My Security Camera Saves 47 Seconds of Footage Every Night at 2:13 AM. I Have Never Seen It Happen. | True Scary Story

scenes.narration: My security camera saves a clip every night at two thirteen AM. Forty seven seconds. Always.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a smartphone screen showing a security camera app timeline with a saved clip marker highlighted at the timestamp two thirteen AM, the clip duration shown as forty seven seconds, dim ambient bedroom light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Wired doorbell camera. Front porch. Installed it myself in March.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a small modern doorbell camera mounted on a wooden door frame, a single LED indicator glowing dim red, an overhead porch light off, evening shadow across the door, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Motion triggered. Saves a clip when something crosses the porch.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a smartphone screen showing a security camera settings page with motion detection enabled, a list of recent clip thumbnails visible below, dim ambient light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Three weeks in I noticed the pattern. Same time every night.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a laptop screen showing a calendar view of saved clips, each day marked with a single clip at the same time stamp in a vertical row, dim desk lamp light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Two thirteen AM. Forty seven seconds. To the frame.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a smartphone showing the saved clip detail page, the start time stamp and duration shown precisely, a thumbnail of a dark porch visible at the top of the screen, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: First time I watched the clip the porch was empty. No motion in frame.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a smartphone playing security camera footage of a dim residential porch at night, a wooden door and a small bench visible, no figure or motion, the timestamp visible in the corner, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: But something triggered it. Forty seven seconds of empty porch every single night.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a laptop screen showing two frames from the security clip side by side, the porch identical in both, dim ambient desk light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I set my alarm for two ten AM. Sat by the window. Waited.
scenes.image_prompt: wide interior shot of a dim living room at night, a single floor lamp glowing low, a person seated in a chair facing a curtained window from behind, no face visible, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Two thirteen passed. Nothing moved on the porch. The clip saved anyway.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a smartphone screen showing a new saved clip notification timestamped two thirteen AM, the phone resting on a windowsill, dim ambient light from a lamp behind, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Last week I watched the clip frame by frame. Found one frame.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a laptop screen showing a single frame of security footage isolated in a video editor timeline, the frame counter visible, dim desk lamp light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Frame two hundred eighty nine of fourteen hundred. A face in the porch window. From outside.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a laptop screen showing a single isolated security camera frame of a residential porch window at night, the reflection on the glass showing the indistinct outline of a face pressed close from the exterior side, dim screen glow, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I do not have a porch window. The camera is the only thing facing the street.
scenes.image_prompt: wide exterior shot of a residential front porch at dusk, a solid wooden door and a small bench, no window visible on this side of the house, a small doorbell camera mounted near the door, vertical 9:16 framing
